core_mechanism: self-consistent mean-field reduction of a high-dimensional, randomly-coupled interacting system to a single effective element driven by a self-generated noise field, closed by a self-consistency condition on the field's statistics, with the onset of distinct regimes fixed by linear-stability/bifurcation thresholds of the reduced low-dimensional description
brief_reason: The abstract builds a dynamical mean-field theory that collapses a large random recurrent system into an effective self-consistent single-unit stochastic process and locates its regime changes through a chaos-onset instability and a Hopf bifurcation captured by a reduced three-dimensional model.
